wazuh-mcp.action: flagging one critical alert on a Wazuh SIEM dashboard: a table of alert rows with severity dots, red for critical, orange for high, blue for medium, green for low, plus a small donut chart; the owl marks the top red CRITICAL row

misp-mcp.action: correlating threat intelligence on a MISP board: a few labelled IOC attribute cards (DOMAIN, IP, HASH) joined into a small correlation graph; the owl ties two related indicators together, the link lines glowing cyan

suricata-mcp.action: reviewing a Suricata IDS alert feed: a few EVE JSON alert lines each with a signature ID and a severity flag, one alert highlighted red as a freshly triggered signature; the owl marks the red alert

thehive-mcp.action: working a TheHive case board: a small kanban of case and task cards with status chips, one urgent case card in amber-orange; the owl slides a task card to the next column

cortex-mcp.action: running an observable through Cortex analyzers: one observable card feeding a row of analyzer result chips marked safe, suspicious, malicious in green, amber, red; the owl reads the malicious red result

mitre-mcp.character: a great horned owl analyst in a slim cyan visor
mitre-mcp.action: marking one highlighted cell on a MITRE ATT&CK Navigator board: a grid of small technique cells under tactic column headers (RECONNAISSANCE, INITIAL ACCESS, EXECUTION, PERSISTENCE, PRIVILEGE ESCALATION, DEFENSE EVASION, and more), the cells color-coded as a coverage heatmap in muted red, amber, and green; the owl marks one cell labelled TECHNIQUE

zeek-mcp.action: tracing one connection across a sparse Zeek network map of a few labelled nodes (SENSOR, NODE A, NODE B, CLIENT) joined by one highlighted cyan flow line, beside two or three tab-separated log rows labelled conn, dns, http

maltego-mcp.action: running an OSINT link analysis: a Maltego style entity graph of a few labelled nodes (SUBJECT, EMAIL, DOMAIN, IP) joined by edges, redacted placeholder values only, the active link drawn in cyan; the owl links two entities

cyberbrief.action: compiling a one page threat brief: a BLUF briefing sheet with a bottom-line-up-front header and a couple of ATT&CK technique tags, one severity badge in red; the owl stamps the brief

intel-workbench.action: filling an Analysis of Competing Hypotheses matrix on a wall board: a grid with hypothesis column headers (HYP A, HYP B, HYP C, HYP D) and evidence rows (EVID 01 to EVID 05), cells marked consistent with a green check and inconsistent with a red cross; the owl holds one slim pen in a natural grip and marks one highlighted cell

hotwash.action: walking a SOC incident-response playbook: a flowchart of a few connected decision boxes and arrows, one response branch highlighted cyan; the owl traces the path with a feather

n8n-ops-mcp.action: tracing an n8n automation canvas: a few rounded node boxes joined by smooth curved connector wires, the trigger node and one action node highlighted in red-pink; the owl follows the wire from trigger to action

librenms-mcp.action: reading a LibreNMS device panel: a device list with a row of port-status squares, up in green and one down in red, beside a small bandwidth line graph; the owl acknowledges the red down-port

watchtower.action: watching a NOC topology dashboard: a small network map with node status dots, green for up and one red for down, and a thin metrics strip; the owl spots the red node

eero-cli.action: managing an eero mesh network: a few rounded white mesh nodes joined by thin cyan signal arcs and a short client device list, one node highlighted; the owl taps a node

proxmox-mcp.action: inspecting one ethernet cable at a 19 inch homelab rack: two 2U servers with rows of small drive bays and status LEDs, a 24 port switch, and a firewall box; the patch cables plug only into the front RJ45 port row of the switch, neatly seated, and never enter the chassis sides or the servers; a small side screen shows a Proxmox node and VM tree in orange; the owl holds and looks at one ethernet cable

adguard-mcp.action: reviewing an AdGuard Home query log: a list of DNS request rows, most green for allowed and one red for blocked, beside a ring chart of blocked versus allowed; the owl blocks the one red request

proxguard.action: auditing a Proxmox host against a CIS benchmark checklist: a list of check rows with pass in green and fail in red; the owl flags one failed control

soc-stack.action: standing in front of and beside a SOC architecture board, pointing a wingtip at the pipeline block: a clean block diagram of labelled components, sensors, pipeline, MCP, playbooks, joined by arrows, one data path highlighted cyan; the wing stays in front of the board

jellyfin-mcp.action: curating a Jellyfin media library: a wall of a few media poster cards and a thin playback session bar, one poster glowing in purple-blue as the single pop; the owl starts playback on one

immich-mcp.action: curating an Immich photo library: a tidy grid of a few photo thumbnails, one selected portrait thumbnail carrying a small FACE tag chip (the FACE tag sits only on a photo that shows a human face), and one thumbnail glowing in warm multicolor as the single pop; the owl pins one favorite

portgrid.action: at a switch port grid: a neat matrix of small square ports color-coded up in green, down in grey, and error in red; the owl marks one red error port

cutsheet.action: reviewing a network config cutsheet: a before-and-after device config diff with green added lines and red removed lines, one changed line circled; the owl flags the risky change

samba-ad-migration.action: migrating Windows AD to Samba: a domain tree of labelled share folders with an arrow from an AD side to a Samba side, one share folder in transit highlighted cyan; the owl carries a share across

vervet.action: threat-hunting across hosts: a list of hosts with per-host risk scores, one host flagged high-risk in red; the owl circles the risky host
